description: You are a Data Scientist at an aerospace maintenance firm. Develop a predictive maintenance model for a fleet of turbofan engines using the NASA CMAPSS dataset in /home/ga/RProjects/datasets/CMAPSS/. Predict the Remaining Useful Life (RUL) at the last cycle for each engine in test_FD001.txt using train_FD001.txt (columns: Engine_ID, Cycle, Setting1-3, Sensor1-21) for training and RUL_FD001.txt for evaluation. Your analysis must include at least one engineered rolling or lagged feature. Save your complete R script to /home/ga/RProjects/rul_analysis.R. Export test set predictions (Engine_ID and Predicted_RUL for 100 engines) to /home/ga/RProjects/output/rul_predictions.csv. Save the Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) to /home/ga/RProjects/output/model_metrics.csv with columns 'metric' and 'value'. Save a scatter plot of Predicted vs. Actual RUL to /home/ga/RProjects/output/rul_performance_plot.png. Login: username=ga, password=password123.

metadata.task_note: Requires creating time-series features (rolling/lag) and training a regression model (e.g., Random Forest) in R.
